title: World War II: mobilization
description: Test your knowledge on how American participation in World War II changed the American labor force!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb73f35700bc24a0124f39c8221d74e5.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/74/Billboard_1944_Oak_Ridge_%2813427656474%29.jpg) 1944

**This poster most directly reflects which of the following domestic changes in the United States in the 1940s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the mass mobilization of American society to support the war effort
the lack of support for labor union protests in major factories
the use of the draft for the first time to send troops to undermanned battlefronts
the passage of the GI Bill to support veterans of World War II

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb73f35700bc24a0124f39c8221d74e5.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/74/Billboard_1944_Oak_Ridge_%2813427656474%29.jpg) 1944

**Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the billboard?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increase in military production to support the Allies
the end of the Great Depression
the orchestrated Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor
the surrender of German forces

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb73f35700bc24a0124f39c8221d74e5.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/74/Billboard_1944_Oak_Ridge_%2813427656474%29.jpg) 1944

**The image most strongly supports which of the following arguments about domestic life during World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States’ homefront changed to support the war effort by mobilizing a number of Americans to fill jobs in crucial wartime industries.
The United States’ homefront was not affected by World War II as the war was based in Europe and Asia, not the United States.
The United States’ economic situation worsened as its involvement in World War II increased.
The United States encouraged able-bodied men to continue their work on the homefront rather than joining the military.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed5aada1f727afb4c2be870c0b863730.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%22Blueprint_for_victory.%22_-_NARA_-_513975.jpg), between 1941 and 1945

**Which of the following best describes the purpose of this poster?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to encourage women to support the war effort with their labor
to convince women to join labor unions like Industrial Workers of the World
to advocate for the Equal Rights Amendment
to persuade women to pursue engineering degrees in college

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed5aada1f727afb4c2be870c0b863730.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%22Blueprint_for_victory.%22_-_NARA_-_513975.jpg), between 1941 and 1945

**The poster is best seen as evidence for which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how women became a primary labor force during World War II, subverting traditional gender roles
how propaganda appealed to women’s traditional roles in the home to gain their support during the war
how the government aimed to reduce labor union membership by appealing to their patriotism
how the women’s rights movement’s “Double V Campaign” gained traction during the war

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed5aada1f727afb4c2be870c0b863730.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%22Blueprint_for_victory.%22_-_NARA_-_513975.jpg), between 1941 and 1945

**Which of the following developments is the most direct effect of the situation portrayed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a feminist movement in the 1960s that fought for women’s right to work
a feminist movement in the 1980s that sought to institute gender-specific schooling
a social movement in the 1970s that pushed for the legalization of abortion
a social movement in the 1980s that urged states not to ratify the Equal Rights Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
> “But hardships are part of war, and war is an aggregation of hardships. All citizens alike, both in and out of uniform, feel the impact of war in greater or lesser measure. Citizenship has its responsibilities, as well as its privileges, and, in time of war, the burden is always heavier. Compulsory exclusion of large groups of citizens from their homes, except under circumstances of direst emergency and peril, is inconsistent with our basic governmental institutions. But when, under conditions of modern warfare, our shores are threatened by hostile forces, the power to protect must be commensurate with the threatened danger.”

>-Source: Associate Justice Hugo Black, *Korematsu v. United States,* the opinion of the Court, 1944

**The *Korematsu* case emerged most directly from the context of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the internment of Japanese-Americans after an organized attack of a military base in Hawaii
the repatriation of people of Mexican descent to reduce government spending during World War II
the passage of Selective Training and Service Act of 1940 that required all men from 21 to 45 to register for the draft
the imprisonment of suspected Communists and Communist sympathizers throughout the war

assessment_items.question_content:
> “But hardships are part of war, and war is an aggregation of hardships. All citizens alike, both in and out of uniform, feel the impact of war in greater or lesser measure. Citizenship has its responsibilities, as well as its privileges, and, in time of war, the burden is always heavier. Compulsory exclusion of large groups of citizens from their homes, except under circumstances of direst emergency and peril, is inconsistent with our basic governmental institutions. But when, under conditions of modern warfare, our shores are threatened by hostile forces, the power to protect must be commensurate with the threatened danger.”

>-Source: Associate Justice Hugo Black, *Korematsu v. United States,* the opinion of the Court, 1944

**The federal government established its legal authority to address threats considered a clear and present danger during which of the following earlier periods?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: *Schenck v. United States* (1919)
*Plessy v. Ferguson* (1896)
*Dred Scott v. Sandford* (1854)
*Marbury v. Madison* (1803)

assessment_items.question_content:
> “But hardships are part of war, and war is an aggregation of hardships. All citizens alike, both in and out of uniform, feel the impact of war in greater or lesser measure. Citizenship has its responsibilities, as well as its privileges, and, in time of war, the burden is always heavier. Compulsory exclusion of large groups of citizens from their homes, except under circumstances of direst emergency and peril, is inconsistent with our basic governmental institutions. But when, under conditions of modern warfare, our shores are threatened by hostile forces, the power to protect must be commensurate with the threatened danger.”

>-Source: Associate Justice Hugo Black, *Korematsu v. United States,* the opinion of the Court, 1944

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how the United States government’s power to restrict certain civil liberties is greatly expanded during wartime
how the Supreme Court’s rulings often aim to protect minority rights, rather than restrict them
how the United States government sought to open its borders to Jewish refugees from German-occupied countries
how the Supreme Court’s rulings reflect a commitment to individual liberty, even during times of war

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1a6dbe6810b83d9eb161b2731c32bf2.svg)

>-Source: U.S. Census Bureau, [Historical Statistics of the United States, 1789 to 1945](https://www2.census.gov/library/publications/1949/compendia/hist_stats_1789-1945/hist_stats_1789-1945-chB.pdf?#), 1949

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1935 to 1945 as shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the need for low-cost labor to meet war production goals
the desire to reunite families during World War II
the growing political unrest leading to the Mexican Revolution
the rising temperature, making it impossible to grow crops

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1a6dbe6810b83d9eb161b2731c32bf2.svg)

>-Source: U.S. Census Bureau, [Historical Statistics of the United States, 1789 to 1945](https://www2.census.gov/library/publications/1949/compendia/hist_stats_1789-1945/hist_stats_1789-1945-chB.pdf?#), 1949

**Which of the following best explains the changes in the population of Mexican immigrants from 1935 to 1945?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of the *Bracero* program
the enactment of a policy of Mexican repatriation
the start of the Mexican Revolution, causing human rights violations in Mexico
the extension of citizenship to immigrants who joined the American military

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1a6dbe6810b83d9eb161b2731c32bf2.svg)

>-Source: U.S. Census Bureau, [Historical Statistics of the United States, 1789 to 1945](https://www2.census.gov/library/publications/1949/compendia/hist_stats_1789-1945/hist_stats_1789-1945-chB.pdf?#), 1949

**The migrants represented by the graph most typically settled in which of the following regions of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the West and Southwest
the Northwest
the Northeast
the East and Southeast
